title: tripleurospermum perforatum
intro: Tripleurospermum perforatum, commonly known as stinking chamomile, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of stinking chamomile include soothing digestive issues, reducing anxiety and stress, and relieving minor skin irritations. Stinking chamomile can be prepared into various herbal teas, infusions, and salves, making it a popular remedy for a range of health issues.
